Migration Guide

Upgrade your project to the latest Storyhouse versions and migrate between major releases.

Overview

This guide documents breaking changes, deprecations, and migration paths between Storyhouse versions. Always review the relevant section before upgrading.

v0.1.0 (Alpha) to v0.2.0 (Beta)

Planned — no breaking changes released yet

The current release (v0.1.0) is an alpha preview. The following changes are planned for the beta release and may require migration work:

Component Renames (Planned)

Current Name New Name Reason
Card Unchanged —
Button Unchanged —
Input TextField Considered Align with form field naming conventions

Package Restructuring (Planned)

  • @storyhouse/components will remain the main component package
  • @storyhouse/tokens will gain CSS variable output as the default export (currently requires explicit /tokens.css path)
  • @storyhouse/utils will export cn() as the default export

Migration Steps (v0.1 → v0.2)

  1. Update imports if Card or Input are renamed. Use search-and-replace across your codebase:
    Terminal 
    # If Input becomes TextField
find src/ -type f -name '*.tsx' -exec sed -i 's/\bInput\b/TextField/g' {} +
  2. Update token imports if path changes:
    tokens-import.diff 
    - import '@storyhouse/tokens/tokens.css';
+ import '@storyhouse/tokens';
  3. Test all forms for any validation behavior changes

API Compatibility Notes

Button

The Button API is stable in v0.1.0. No prop changes are planned for the beta release. All current props (variant, size, isLoading, fullWidth, disabled) will continue working.

Card

The Card composable sub-component pattern (CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent, CardFooter) is stable. The variant and padding props are final.

Input

The Input component may be renamed to TextField in v0.2.0 to better distinguish it from other form field types (Select, Textarea, etc.) that will be added in future releases. If this change happens, the old Input export will be deprecated with a warning for one minor version before removal.

Badge

Badge is stable. The Six-variant system (default, primary, success, warning, danger, neutral) is final. dot and removable props are stable.

Avatar

Avatar is stable. The five-size system (xs through xl) and two-shape system (circle, square) are final. The deterministic color algorithm is considered part of the public API — changing it would cause visual inconsistency across sessions.

Upgrade Checklist

When upgrading between any major versions, follow this checklist:

  1. Read the changelog for the target version
  2. Review the migration guide for breaking changes
  3. Update package.json version numbers
  4. Run npm install to update dependencies
  5. Run your test suite to catch regressions
  6. Run the build to verify no compilation errors
  7. Visually smoke-test all component pages in your app
  8. Check dark mode rendering for each component
  9. Verify Storybook renders correctly with the new version

Handling Legacy Code

If you're maintaining an older codebase and cannot immediately migrate, you can pin to a specific version in package.json:

package.json 
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@storyhouse/components": "0.1.x",
    "@storyhouse/tokens": "0.1.x"
  }
}

The 0.1.x range will receive patch updates (bug fixes) without breaking changes. Minor version bumps (0.2.0, 0.3.0) may include breaking changes until v1.0.0.

v1.0.0 Roadmap

The v1.0.0 release will mark the stable, production-ready version of Storyhouse. Planned changes from alpha/v0.x:

  • All component APIs are frozen (no breaking changes after v1.0)
  • Tree-shakeable imports for smaller bundles
  • Full CSS custom property coverage for all component colors
  • Stable theme / dark mode API
  • Semver compliance — breaking changes only in major versions
  • Long-term support policy documentation
ESC
